source.description: Two real installed agent clients independently completed the same disposable synthetic stdio MCP task through the production Pinpoint CLI gateway.
source.persistedData: Only content-free receipts are retained. Client event streams, debug logs, provider credentials, personal paths, and temporary MCP state are deleted after grading.

fixture.task: Find one email by accountId through an MCP server and return only the value.
fixture.upstreamConstraint: The synthetic upstream tool intentionally exposes no filtering parameter and returns 1,000 records under structuredContent.data.accounts.

hosts.reason: The installed client is not authenticated. No task or provider request was run.
The installed client has an incompatible local xhigh setting; an earlier low-effort override reached the provider but returned 401 Unauthorized. No result is counted.

limitations: This is one shared first-party synthetic task across two clients, not a prevalence estimate or customer-production trace.
Both prompts explicitly required the accounts MCP server. Claude denied unrelated tools; Copilot exposed only the accounts server.
The upstream server intentionally lacked filtering, representing a poorly bounded tool rather than every MCP server.

Claude's 508-character value measures model-visible tool-result content. Copilot's 2,840-character value is a conservative complete JSONL tool-completion event size and includes event metadata; they are not directly comparable.
Character reduction is not provider token or bill reduction. Extra discovery and query turns remain part of net cost.
Cursor and Codex are explicitly not counted as passes.
